question: What is the definition of the abbreviation CNV?

nuscale_ch01_0023.title: Containment Systems
nuscale_ch01_0023.summary: The NuScale containment vessel (CNV) is maintained under vacuum during normal operation, which reduces moisture and corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits oxygen for safety. The design relies on passive safety systems and eliminates large external piping, resulting in improved safety, lower core damage frequency, and a smaller radioactive source term compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates at full power using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for pumps. Its small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design features passive safety systems, dedicated emergency cooling, and decay heat removal, all housed in a steel containment partially immersed in water for effective heat management. The NPM allows for flexible power adjustments and core maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system monitoring and operation, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and devices such as flow meters, pressure and temperature sensors, sample probes, and level indicators. It serves as a reference to interpret the P&ID drawings accurately by defining each symbol and its application within the system.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section B provides symbol legends and abbreviations, which may include the definition of 'CNV', thus serving as a potential source for answering the analysis question posed to Section A.
Section B provides diagram legends that may define abbreviations such as CNV, which could be referenced or used in Section A's description of containment components.
Section A and Section B both provide symbol legends for instrumentation and piping diagrams, which are sources where the definition of the abbreviation CNV may be found.
